rationale: The query/hyp method is a linear-stability analysis of a two-state diffusively-coupled dynamical system on a random graph -- linearizing about a homogeneous state, projecting onto graph-Laplacian eigenmodes to get a dispersion relation, and finding a degree-dependent bifurcation into clusters with a mean-field closure over degree classes. The candidate instead presents a static generative random-graph model with tunable clustering/transitivity, solved via generating functions for component sizes, giant-component onset, and percolation thresholds -- there is no dynamics, no activator/inhibitor-like two-state coupling, no eigenmode dispersion relation, and no bifurcation/hysteresis, so the core machinery does not match. The object also differs: the query studies emergent activator/inhibitor pattern formation (a dynamical process) on a network, whereas the candidate studies purely structural/topological properties (clustering coefficient, percolation phase transition) of a graph-generation model, so despite sharing the general 'networks' vocabulary these are not the same object or phenomenon.
